changes.before: Shelves, crates, a bench. Storage room.
changes.after: Shelves, crates, a bench: storage room.

changes.before: No guard. No barricade.
changes.after: The approach stood open—unguarded, unbarricaded.

changes.before: Work voice. Too even.
changes.after: Her work voice came out too even.

changes.before: Scholarly order sat over butcher's work, making the cuts look methodical.
changes.after: The cuts were neat, methodical.

changes.before: Worse: as if she had stayed.
changes.after: Worse: she stood like she belonged there.

changes.before: Colder than betrayal: a weakness she could not ignore.
changes.after: A weakness she could not ignore.

changes.before: He meant it.
changes.after: There was no strain in his voice.

changes.before: Wise.
That detail would have meant nothing outside this room. Here it meant too much.
changes.after: Here, the chalk mattered.

changes.before: With Kel'Thuzad dead, the wards and routines kept cycling on their own.
changes.after: Even dead, Kel'Thuzad left the wards cycling.

changes.before: The discard rooms had not changed.
changes.after: Hooks. Vats. Parts laid out by category.

changes.before: One more place a fourth hand should have been.
changes.after: Automatic, and still built for four.

changes.before: This was what the side of the building was for.
changes.after: The side route led straight into the work.

changes.before: One last useful fact, given before the body failed.
changes.after: He gave one last useful fact before he folded.

changes.before: The plainness of it hit harder than a sneer.
changes.after: His plain delivery hit harder than a sneer.

changes.before: Sylvanas had her answer: Jaina was still useful and dangerous, but structure could hold her still longer than safety allowed.
changes.after: Sylvanas had her answer: Jaina was still useful and dangerous, but Kel'Thuzad could keep her there longer by feeding her structure than safety allowed.

changes.before: Silence came down over the sanctum.
changes.after: The sanctum went silent.

changes.before: Jaina kept pace easily enough, but the silence around her carried a different weight now.
changes.after: Jaina kept pace easily enough, but the quiet around her felt heavier now.

changes.before: A lecturer finishing a point.
changes.after: Kel'Thuzad still sounded like he was lecturing.

changes.before: A scholar's office buried under stone and bones.
changes.after: Books, boards, and specimen jars filled the stone room.

changes.before: No good time to say that aloud. Never.
changes.after: She kept that to herself.

changes.before: Hearthglen was overwhelmed, and Stratholme was next.
changes.after: If Hearthglen had broken, Stratholme came next.

changes.before: They fell back into line by habit, but the spacing stayed wrong.
changes.after: They fell back into line by habit, but the old spacing never came back.

changes.before: Jaina stood three strides off the boards, not near the door, not with her staff raised, not standing like a captive.
changes.after: Jaina stood three strides off the boards, away from the door, staff down, like she belonged in the room.

changes.before: Not touch-close. Escort-close.
changes.after: Escort-close.
